summary: Synthetic support interaction with misleading evidence, abandoned paths, conflicting observations, a hypothesis reversal, and a late root-cause reveal.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: I need a fast answer here. customer reports symptoms consistent with more than one migration issue.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: I think this may be a login thing, but let's see. When did this first start?

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: the issue began after a recent operational change

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: That points me toward timezone conversion, but I want to keep this tentative. What changed right before the issue started?

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: The confusing part is this: An early symptom seems to support timezone conversion, but later evidence weakens it.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Let's test timezone conversion first. If that fits, we should see the same behavior on a known-good example.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: We checked one example and it seemed to fit, but then another example did not. customer reports symptoms consistent with more than one migration issue, but at least one comparison case behaves differently.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: That weakens timezone conversion. I am going to abandon that path for now and check duplicate merge.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: duplicate merge happened to us once before, so that sounds plausible, but the symptoms are not exactly the same.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Agreed. duplicate merge is possible, but the evidence is mixed. I need a comparison between a working and blocked case.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: One working case has the same visible setup. Also, an admin later mentions inactive owner exclusion.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: That reverses the direction. The visible setup may not be the control point. I want to inspect the shared configuration boundary.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: I just got a note from someone else: an admin later mentions inactive owner exclusion. customer has a time-sensitive rollout or business deadline and trust is degrading.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: That late detail finally makes the actual path coherent. The likely root cause is: The migration problem is caused by inactive owner exclusion.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: So the earlier checks were not wasted, but they were pointing at the wrong control point?

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Correct. The earlier paths were plausible but abandoned after conflicting evidence. Final outcome: agent identifies inactive owner exclusion as the likely operational cause, but certainty depends on the selected resolution type
